truth_matrix_role: support-surface coverage artifact; placeholder tier statuses remain there by design, so closeout judgments live in this exit matrix

destinations.name: filesystem
destinations.supported_subset: one shared delivery payload per local artifact write, explicit output-root subset, shared replay compatibility
destinations.recovery_replay_idempotency: path failure normalization, crash-adjacent overwrite recovery, replay success, duplicate replay stability, batch/worker consistency
destinations.shared_semantics_proven: shared receipt summary, retryability/failure kind/error code, batch_report alignment, delivery provenance
residual_risks: Bounded to explicit output-root semantics; not a general file sync or transactional multi-writer sink.

destinations.name: webhook
destinations.supported_subset: one shared payload per HTTP attempt, explicit endpoint/header subset, bounded response normalization, shared replay sink path
destinations.recovery_replay_idempotency: success, retryable 503, non-retryable 400, replay success, duplicate replay stability, batch/worker consistency
destinations.shared_semantics_proven: receipt summary, batch_report, by-destination totals, replay provenance, DLQ/replay alignment
residual_risks: Current retained subset does not define a provider-owned auth workflow; authenticity is proven on the bounded endpoint/header subset instead.

destinations.name: sqlite
destinations.supported_subset: one shared payload per bounded row-style write, explicit database/table subset, shared replay sink path
destinations.recovery_replay_idempotency: success, lock-sensitive failure, replay success, duplicate replay stability, batch/worker consistency
destinations.shared_semantics_proven: shared receipt classification, replay provenance, shared payload ownership through sqlite replay sink
residual_risks: Bounded to explicit table/write subset; not a generalized SQL transaction coordinator.

destinations.name: kafka
destinations.supported_subset: one shared payload per bounded message write, explicit topic/key subset, shared replay sink path
destinations.recovery_replay_idempotency: success, transport/broker failure classification, replay success, truthful duplicate replay append behavior, batch/worker consistency
destinations.shared_semantics_proven: shared receipt vocabulary, retryability and error-code normalization, replay provenance stability
residual_risks: Current Redpanda runtime is plaintext, so auth failure is not truthfully testable there.
Kafka duplicate behavior is append-style within the retained subset; no exact-once claim is made.

destinations.name: weaviate
destinations.supported_subset: one shared payload per bounded object write, explicit collection/object-id subset, shared replay sink path
destinations.recovery_replay_idempotency: success, auth/config failure, retryable transport failure, replay success, duplicate replay truthfully constrained by backend object identity
destinations.shared_semantics_proven: shared failure mapping, provenance continuity, batch/worker consistency
residual_risks: Duplicate behavior is provider constraint/id-based, not a generic backend exact-once guarantee.

destinations.name: s3
destinations.supported_subset: one shared payload per object write, explicit bucket plus prefix subset, identity-key-based object naming, shared replay sink path
destinations.recovery_replay_idempotency: success, missing-bucket/config failure, auth failure, retryable endpoint failure, replay success, duplicate replay stability
destinations.shared_semantics_proven: shared receipt vocabulary, replay provenance, batch/worker consistency
residual_risks: Bounded to one-object-per-payload writes; no bulk manifest or object-collection workflow claim.

destinations.name: opensearch
destinations.supported_subset: one shared payload per document write, explicit index plus document-id subset, shared replay sink path
destinations.recovery_replay_idempotency: success, auth/config failure, retryable connection failure, replay success, duplicate replay stability
destinations.shared_semantics_proven: trust_env=False HTTP probing, shared receipt classification, replay provenance continuity
residual_risks: Bounded to explicit index/document-id writes; no cluster-admin, bulk job, or async workflow semantics.

destinations.name: clickhouse
destinations.supported_subset: one shared payload per row-style write, explicit table/write-mode subset, shared replay sink path
destinations.recovery_replay_idempotency: success, auth failure, missing-table non-retryable failure, retryable connection failure, replay success, duplicate replay truthfully deduped by retained idempotent subset
destinations.shared_semantics_proven: shared failure mapping including real missing-table classification, replay provenance stability, batch/worker consistency
residual_risks: Bounded to explicit row-style write subset; no async bulk export/import or transaction workflow claim.

destinations.name: mongodb
destinations.supported_subset: one shared payload per document replace/upsert, explicit database plus collection subset, identity-key-based document selection, shared replay sink path
destinations.recovery_replay_idempotency: success, auth failure, retryable connection failure, replay success, duplicate replay/upsert stability
destinations.shared_semantics_proven: shared receipt vocabulary, replay provenance, batch/worker consistency
residual_risks: Bounded to replace/upsert subset; no broader multi-document transaction or oplog-owned recovery semantics.

destinations.name: loki
destinations.supported_subset: one shared payload per bounded log push, explicit stream/tenant label subset, shared replay sink path
destinations.recovery_replay_idempotency: success, tenant/auth failure, retryable transport failure, replay success, duplicate replay audited truthfully without exact-once claim
destinations.shared_semantics_proven: shared receipt vocabulary, retryability calibration, replay provenance continuity
residual_risks: Loki remains append-style within the retained subset; duplicate semantics are audited truthfully, not normalized into a false dedupe guarantee.

sources.name: http_json_cursor_v1
sources.supported_subset: explicit URL plus query subset, one cursor parameter, JSON-object response subset, optional next-cursor continuation, state-only resume within cursor_v1
sources.recovery_or_partition_evidence: real cursor progression, checkpoint creation, state-only resume continuation, task identity stability, resumed provenance continuity
sources.failure_coverage: success, retryable transport failure, non-retryable malformed-response/config failure; auth is not part of the current bounded fixture path
sources.identity_provenance_secrets: source identity and resumed provenance proven; no secret-bearing fields are promoted into task identity
residual_risks: Record-level continuation remains outside the supported subset.
The bounded fixture path does not provide a separate real auth surface.

sources.name: postgresql_incremental_v1
sources.supported_subset: ordered incremental table reads over one ascending cursor column, explicit selected columns, optional starting cursor, bounded batch size, cursor_v1 resume
sources.recovery_or_partition_evidence: real cursor progression, checkpoint creation, checkpoint lineage, resume tail continuation, shared ingest visibility for resumed provenance
sources.failure_coverage: success, auth/config failure, retryable connection failure, non-retryable missing-table/query failure
sources.identity_provenance_secrets: task identity stability, checkpoint identity distinctness, resumed provenance consistency proven; credentials do not affect identity
residual_risks: Bounded to one ascending cursor-column subset; no CDC, transaction-session resume, or broader relational extraction semantics.

sources.name: clickhouse_incremental_v1
sources.supported_subset: ordered table/query reads over one ascending cursor column, explicit selected columns, bounded batch size, cursor_v1 resume
sources.recovery_or_partition_evidence: real cursor progression, checkpoint creation, lineage and resume-tail continuity
sources.failure_coverage: success, auth/config failure, retryable connection failure, non-retryable missing-table/query failure
sources.identity_provenance_secrets: task identity stability, checkpoint identity distinctness, resumed provenance continuity proven
residual_risks: Bounded to ordered warehouse query subset; no async export/job workflow semantics.

sources.name: kafka_partition_offset_v1
sources.supported_subset: explicit topic plus one partition, ascending offset progression, optional starting offset, bounded batch size, JSON-object payload subset, cursor_v1 resume
sources.recovery_or_partition_evidence: real offset progression, checkpoint creation, resume from partition-offset state, lineage and resumed provenance continuity
sources.failure_coverage: success, retryable broker-unreachable failure, non-retryable JSON decode/config failure; auth is not truthfully testable on the current plaintext runtime
sources.identity_provenance_secrets: source identity stability and checkpoint identity distinctness proven; transient broker sessions do not affect identity
residual_risks: Bounded to one partition and explicit offset slice; no consumer-group, rebalance, or multi-partition semantics.
Plaintext runtime means auth failure is not a truthfully testable current surface.

sources.name: mongodb_incremental_v1
sources.supported_subset: ordered collection reads over one ascending cursor field, explicit projected fields, optional starting cursor, bounded batch size, cursor_v1 resume
sources.recovery_or_partition_evidence: real cursor progression, checkpoint creation, lineage and resume-tail continuity
sources.failure_coverage: success, auth/config failure, retryable connection failure, non-retryable document-shape/missing-collection failure
sources.identity_provenance_secrets: source identity, checkpoint identity, resumed provenance, and ownership boundaries proven
residual_risks: Bounded to ordered collection-read subset; no oplog, change-stream, or token-owned recovery semantics.

sources.name: http_document_v1
sources.authenticity_level: service-live against runtime-backed HTTP fixture plus real partition path
sources.supported_subset: explicit document URL, bounded request-header subset, one fetched document per task, inferred filename/mime subset, direct partition handoff
sources.recovery_or_partition_evidence: real fetched artifact enters partition path; M7 proves reacquisition and repartition facts remain inspectable through shared ingest surfaces
sources.failure_coverage: success, retryable transport/timeout failure, non-retryable 404 or unsupported response; no separate real auth model in the bounded fixture path
sources.identity_provenance_secrets: source identity stability, provenance correctness, and secret non-leakage in metadata proven
residual_risks: Current bounded fixture path does not provide a separate auth surface.
Support remains one-document-per-task; no discovery or long-lived sync semantics.

sources.name: s3_document_v1
sources.supported_subset: explicit bucket plus key selection, optional version id, one fetched object per task, direct partition handoff
sources.recovery_or_partition_evidence: real fetched object enters partition path and yields Zephyr-owned normalized outputs
sources.failure_coverage: success, auth/config failure, retryable endpoint/storage failure, non-retryable missing-object failure
sources.identity_provenance_secrets: source identity stability, provenance correctness, and secret non-leakage proven
residual_risks: Bounded to one explicit object fetch; no prefix listing or multi-object discovery semantics.

sources.name: git_document_v1
sources.supported_subset: explicit repo-root plus commit plus relative-path selection, bounded blob fetch, stable resolved commit/blob provenance subset, one fetched file per task
sources.recovery_or_partition_evidence: real fetched blob enters partition path and preserves Zephyr-owned artifact ownership
sources.failure_coverage: success, non-retryable missing-path/missing-commit/config failure; no truthful auth surface in the bounded local repo-root model
sources.identity_provenance_secrets: source identity stability and provenance correctness proven; secret non-leakage maintained
residual_risks: Current bounded local model does not expose a stable retryable remote-fetch failure case.
Current bounded local model does not expose a truthful auth surface.

sources.name: google_drive_document_v1
sources.supported_subset: explicit file selection, bounded export-or-download acquisition subset, optional drive locator, bounded export mime subset, one fetched file per task
sources.recovery_or_partition_evidence: real SaaS fetch enters the real partition path and preserves Zephyr-owned artifact ownership
sources.failure_coverage: success, auth failure, non-retryable missing-file/config failure; the live harness differentiates transient network retries from true auth/config failures without weakening trust_env=False
sources.identity_provenance_secrets: source identity stability, provenance correctness, and secret non-leakage proven; legacy runtime-home alias env names are mapped without exposing secret values
residual_risks: Dedicated provider-controlled retryable failure is not isolated as a stable separate test surface; transient network interference is bounded in the live harness instead.
Support remains one-file-per-task; no folder traversal or sync semantics.

sources.name: confluence_document_v1
sources.supported_subset: explicit site plus page selection, optional requested page-version subset, bounded page-body fetch, one fetched page per task
sources.recovery_or_partition_evidence: real fetched page body enters partition path and preserves Zephyr-owned artifact ownership
sources.failure_coverage: success, auth failure, retryable transport failure, non-retryable missing-version/config failure
sources.identity_provenance_secrets: source identity stability, provenance correctness, and secret non-leakage proven; email is auth-only and excluded from identity/provenance
residual_risks: Support remains bounded to page-body fetch; no attachment crawling or page-tree recursion semantics.

preserved_input_paths.name: airbyte-message-json
preserved_input_paths.authenticity_level: preserved protocol-path evidence over local-real corpus and recovery-drill
preserved_input_paths.supported_subset: preserved it-stream message-json input path over retained corpus, malformed/unsupported protocol-path handling, cursor_v1-compatible checkpoint/resume where present
preserved_input_paths.protocol_path_evidence: representative corpus success, malformed input failure, unsupported protocol failure, checkpoint creation, resumed cursor provenance, artifact ownership and task identity stability
residual_risks: This remains a preserved input path, not a remote connector or broader Airbyte platform support claim.
Coverage is bounded to the retained corpus/protocol subset already proven in repo.

cross_cutting.name: auth_secrets_env_hardening
cross_cutting.outcome: runtime-home resolution, control-plane/runtime-plane separation, env precedence, redaction, SaaS env detection, and legacy Google Drive alias mapping are explicitly covered
residual_risks: Secrets remain external runtime-home concerns by design; closeout artifacts reference only variable names/categories.

cross_cutting.name: service_live_and_local_real_runtime_smoke
cross_cutting.outcome: shared substrate healthchecks remain green through runtime-home resolution with trust_env=False on HTTP probe paths where applicable

cross_cutting.name: replay_dlq_recovery_redrive_calibration
cross_cutting.outcome: retryable vs non-retryable classification drives the expected replay/DLQ ownership path, replay provenance remains explicit, and representative replay success/failure stay inspectable

cross_cutting.name: runtime_queue_lock_operator_drills
cross_cutting.outcome: queue, lock, runtime, inspect, and requeue-derived operator surfaces are pressured through the real zephyr-ingest ownership model rather than fake orchestration
residual_risks: Current queue/lock/runtime shape remains single-runtime/local-backend oriented, which is explicit and inherited into P5 rather than hidden.

cross_cutting.name: batch_report_governance_summary_calibration
cross_cutting.outcome: shared summary fields remain truthful after representative failure and replay activity: delivery outcome, retryability, failure kind, error code, attempt count, payload count, by-destination totals, and replay-relevant provenance

cross_cutting.name: shared_provenance_and_operator_inspectability
cross_cutting.outcome: shared operator-facing surfaces can distinguish intake vs resume vs replay, while it checkpoint semantics remain local and uns reacquisition/repartition facts remain local but inspectable

cross_cutting.name: control_plane_runtime_plane_separation
cross_cutting.outcome: repo-owned code/tests/helpers remain control-plane assets and real env/compose/secrets remain in the external runtime-home

notes: Baseline and second-round destinations reached the same authenticity bar; evidence form differs only by truthful backend topology (service-live versus local-real/runtime-hardened).
Baseline and later-added sources reached the same authenticity bar; evidence form differs only by truthful source topology (service-live, local-real, saas-live, or preserved protocol-path).
No retained surface is being excused from replay/recovery/provenance or identity discipline because of historical lineage.

non_blocking_residual_risks: Several retained subsets are intentionally bounded and do not claim broader source-state, sync, consumer-group, CDC, attachment-crawl, tree-traversal, transaction, or async bulk-job semantics.

Some auth or dedicated retryable cases remain not truthfully testable where the retained runtime topology does not provide them, but those absences match the explicitly supported subset and do not create dual standards.
SaaS live validation depends on external network reachability and valid runtime-home credentials; the closeout records only the bounded surfaces actually proven without leaking any secret values.
